summary: Histograms are best for representing continuous variables as they show frequency distributions within specific numerical range intervals. Line plots also track changes over sequences like timeseries yet aren't optimal for visualising full spectrum variability present inherently among continuously measured attributes directly comparable through interval-based visualization techniques offered by histograms more effectively conveying statistical properties pertinent medical research applications involving such types of measurement scales. The other options do not suitably represent this kind of data due to limitations noted earlier concerning bar graph usage mainly restricted category counts without gradation nuances required when
